rationale: Both correctly diagnose the ground-truth root cause (default --run-level=core_model forces dummy weights via _add_dummy_load_format, unrelated to the full_model pytest marker) and prescribe the same fix, matching akshatvishu/yenuo26's resolution. X is cleaner and more thorough: it explains why the marker doesn't set run-level, cites a broader/more coherent set of file:line references, and adds concrete preconditions (GPU mem split, video path) plus a well-scoped follow-up suggestion. Y's substance is equally correct but its packaging hurts completeness — it leaks raw internal pipeline metadata ('# Run report', a literal task dict) atop the answer, and its 'Additional context' Fix section is malformed (a 'Run with the correct run-level:' line with no accompanying code block before jumping to the marker-based alternative), making it read less like a polished maintainer reply.
